dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9484 IH: Water Reservoir Transparency Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Scholten (for herself and Mrs. Sykes) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure

form.official-title: To require the Secretary of the Army to conduct a study on the effect of new commercial and industrial water users on the amount of water maintained by Corps of Engineers reservoirs, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Water Reservoir Transparency Act.

section.header: Study on commercial and industrial water use

subsection.text: The Secretary shall conduct a study on the ways in which the operation of new commercial and industrial water users affects the use of and water levels in reservoirs maintained by the Corps of Engineers.
Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall—

paragraph.text: submit to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate a report containing the results of the study conducted under subsection (a); and
make such study publicly available on a website of the Corps of Engineers.

subsection.text: In this section, the term Secretary means the Secretary of the Army, acting through the Chief of Engineers.
